data.content: E _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) = \mathbf { z } \sim q _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { z } \, | \, \mathbf { x } ) = q ( \mathbf { Z } \, | \, f _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) )
data.latex: E _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) = \mathbf { z } \sim q _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { z } \, | \, \mathbf { x } ) = q ( \mathbf { Z } \, | \, f _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) )

data.content: D _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { z } ) = \mathbf { x } \sim p _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { x } \mid \mathbf { z } ) = p ( \mathbf { X } \mid g _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { z } ) )
data.latex: D _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { z } ) = \mathbf { x } \sim p _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { x } \mid \mathbf { z } ) = p ( \mathbf { X } \mid g _ { \theta } ( \mathbf { z } ) )

data.content: E _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) = \mu _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) + \sigma _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) \odot \epsilon , \qquad \epsilon \sim { \mathcal { N } } ( \mathbf { 0 } , \mathbf { I } )
data.latex: E _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) = \mu _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) + \sigma _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) \odot \epsilon , \qquad \epsilon \sim { \mathcal { N } } ( \mathbf { 0 } , \mathbf { I } )

data.content: { \mathcal { L } } _ { \mathrm { R E C } } = \| \mathbf { x } - \mu _ { \theta } ( E _ { \phi } ( \mathbf { x } ) ) \| _ { 2 } ^ { 2 }

data.content:
The formulas in the image can be written in LaTeX as follows:

```latex
\mathcal{L}_{\text{REC}} = -\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z} \sim q_\phi(\mathbf{z} \mid \mathbf{x})} \log p_\theta(\mathbf{x} \mid \mathbf{z})
\qquad
\mathcal{L}_{\text{KL}} = \mathbb{K}\mathbb{L}(q_\phi(\mathbf{z} \mid \mathbf{x}) || p(\mathbf{z}))
```

This will produce the same equations as shown in the image.

data.content:
The formula in the image can be written in LaTeX as follows:

```latex
\arg \min_{\phi, \theta} \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x} \sim p_{\text{data}}} \mathcal{L}_{\text{ELBO}} = \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x} \sim p_{\text{data}}} \mathcal{L}_{\text{REC}} + \mathcal{L}_{\text{KL}}
```

This will render the equation as shown in the image.
